sourceMovie.title: Soylent Green
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Soylent Green (1973) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: What Happened to Monday
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Soylent Green clicked for you, What Happened to Monday (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Soylent Green?
faq.answer: Try What Happened to Monday, Blindness, World War Z—each shares DNA with Soylent Green while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Soylent Green?
faq.answer: Soylent Green (1973) is best described as Science Fiction, Thriller, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Soylent Green worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
